events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a breaker/conductor ampacity mismatch in an industrial electrical installation that has been tested/inspected. This is a non-compliant condition - the overcurrent device doesn't match the conductor ampacity, which is a violation of NEC 240 requirements for overcurrent protection.

Key issues:
- Breaker/conductor ampacity mismatch means the overcurrent protection device rating doesn't properly protect the conductor
- This is a safety hazard - could lead to conductor overheating/fire
- NEC 240 requires proper coordination between conductors and overcurrent devices
- 110.14(C) requires termination temperature ratings to be considered
- Work has been tested/inspected and found defective

Decision: fail (non-compliant and potentially dangerous)
Risk: high (ampacity mismatch can cause overheating, fire)
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: ~90% (tested/inspected stage, but needs rework)
Confidence: high

Let me write the JSON.

  "findings": [
    "Breaker rating does not match conductor ampacity, creating overcurrent protection mismatch",
    "Conductor may be undersized for the selected breaker or breaker oversized for the conductor, risking overheating and fire under fault or overload conditions",
    "Termination temperature rating per 110.14(C) not properly coordinated with conductor ampacity and breaker rating"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Verify conductor size, insulation type, and ampacity against the engineered drawing and equipment tag",
    "Confirm breaker rating matches the conductor ampacity after applying 110.14(C) termination temperature corrections",
    "Replace either the breaker or the conductor to achieve proper ampacity coordination per NEC 240",
    "Re-inspect and re-test after correction before energizing the circuit"
  ],
  "rationale": "A breaker/conductor ampacity mismatch found at the tested/inspected stage is a non-compliant installation. NEC 240 requires overcurrent devices to protect conductors at their ampacity, and 110.14(C) requires termination temperature ratings to be factored into ampacity determination. An oversized breaker on an undersized conductor will not trip before conductor damage occurs, creating a fire and equipment hazard. The work must fail inspection and be reworked.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 240 - Overcurrent Protection",
    "NEC 210.8 - GFCI Protection",
    "NEC 210.12 - AFCI Protection",
    "NEC 110.14(C) - Electrical Connections, Temperature Ratings"
  ]
}
